data.title: DeadLock ransomware uses the Polygon smart contract to evade tracking.
data.description: According to Group-IB monitoring, the DeadLock ransomware family is exploiting the Polygon smart contract to distribute and rotate proxy server addresses to evade security detection. First discovered in July 2025, this malware uses embedded JavaScript code that interacts with the Polygon network within HTML files, leveraging an RPC list as a gateway to obtain attacker-controlled server addresses. This technique is similar to the previously discovered EtherHiding, aiming to build difficult-to-hide covert communication channels using a decentralized ledger. DeadLock currently has at least three variants, with the latest version embedding the encrypted communication application Session for direct communication with victims.

data.title: Bitmine buys $80.57 million worth of ETH from FalconX
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitmine (0xC7EE...Bbb8), owned by Tom Lee (@fundstrat), purchased 24,068 ETH from FalconX, worth $80.57 million.

data.title: CME Group to Launch Cardano, Chainlink, and Stellar Futures on February 9
data.description:

source: https://coincu.com/news/cme-group-to-launch-cardano-chainlink/

data.title: BitMine's Shareholder Meeting to Discuss Major Stock Proposal
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 15, BitMine Chairman Tom Lee announced in an interview with 3PROTV that Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are expected to attend the annual shareholder meeting scheduled for today, January 15. The meeting will include a vote on a proposal to increase the company's authorized shares from 500 million to 50 billion. Tom Lee emphasized that if the proposal is not approved, the company will be unable to issue new shares to purchase more Ethereum or make acquisitions. Addressing concerns about dilution, he stated that the company has never issued shares below net asset value. Additionally, BitMine plans to present its 2026 development roadmap at the meeting, highlighting growth sources beyond staking yields and revealing potential acquisitions of other crypto treasury companies.

data.title: Binance Futures to Transition LITUSDT Contract to Standard USDⓈ-M
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the LITUSDT perpetual contract on Binance Futures is set to transition from pre-market trading to a standard USDⓈ-M LITUSDT Perpetual Contract on 2026-01-15 at 15:30 (UTC). This transition is expected to take up to three hours, depending on price volatility and the availability of a stable index price. During this period, trading functions will remain operational, and open orders and positions will not be canceled.

**Mark Price Transition**

The transition involves a gradual conversion of the mark price from the pre-market trading mark price to the standard mark price calculation. The mark price will be determined using the formula: Mark Price = Median (Price 1, Price 2, Contract Price). A price cap of ±1% will be applied to the mark price, limiting price changes within a ±1% range every second during both the pre-market trading and the transition period.

**Funding Rates**

Once the pre-market trading concludes, the premium index will be introduced, and the funding rate will adhere to the standard perpetual futures contract's rules. The funding rates can fluctuate between a maximum of +2.00% and a minimum of -2.00%.

This transition marks a significant update in the trading of LITUSDT perpetual contracts on Binance Futures, aiming to enhance the trading experience by aligning with standard perpetual futures contract practices. Users are encouraged to stay informed about the trading rules, leverage, margin, and fee structures associated with USDⓈ-M Futures Contracts to optimize their trading strategies.

data.title: Swift completes digital asset interoperability trials with Chainlink and other institutions.
data.description: As part of its collaboration with Chainlink and UBS Asset Management, Swift has achieved milestone interoperability goals with BNP Paribas, Intesa Sanpaolo, and Société Générale, demonstrating how Swift can coordinate tokenized asset transactions across multiple platforms. Furthermore, Swift has recently conducted a series of trials, including: achieving ISO 20022 interoperability between blockchains with HSBC and Ant International; conducting fiat and digital currency settlements with Citi; facilitating digital asset trading exchanges with Northern Trust and the Reserve Bank of Australia; and connecting tokenized assets with UBS Asset Management and Chainlink. Currently, Swift is focusing on collaborating with over 30 global banks to add blockchain-based ledgers to its infrastructure stack to enable 24/7 real-time cross-border payments.

data.title: Silver Market Cap Surpasses Nvidia, Becomes Second Largest Asset Globally
data.description: According to Odaily, data from the 8marketcap website indicates that the market capitalization of silver has exceeded $5 trillion, currently reported at $5.037 trillion. This positions silver once again as the second-largest asset globally, surpassing Nvidia, and trailing only behind gold, which has a market cap of $32.071 trillion. Previously, on January 7, silver had surpassed Nvidia with a market cap of $4.63 trillion to become the world's second-largest asset.

data.title: Binance Alpha will list RollX on January 16th.
data.description: According to official sources, Binance Alpha will launch RollX (ROLL) on January 16th. Eligible users can claim the airdrop using Binance Alpha Points on the Alpha Events page after trading opens. More details will be announced soon.

data.title: Bank of America CEO: Interest-bearing stablecoins could lead to a $6 trillion loss of bank deposits.
data.description: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan stated during the quarterly earnings call that interest-bearing stablecoins could cause a $6 trillion loss of deposits from the banking system and damage the creditworthiness of small and medium-sized enterprises. Citing data from a U.S. Treasury report, Moynihan pointed out that stablecoins have a financial structure similar to money market mutual funds, with their reserves invested in low-risk securities such as short-term Treasury bonds rather than being converted into bank loans. He believes that the widespread adoption of interest-bearing stablecoins will force banks to turn to more expensive wholesale funding, thereby driving up overall borrowing costs. Currently, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee is discussing a draft cryptocurrency bill that proposes to prohibit idle stablecoins from generating interest. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong posted on the X platform that Coinbase has officially withdrawn its support for the bill due to its inclusion of provisions restricting stablecoin rewards, substantially prohibiting tokenized stocks, and restricting DeFi. Armstrong accused the amendments of aiming to eliminate competition for banks by removing stablecoin rewards. As a result, the Senate Banking Committee has postponed its vote, originally scheduled for January 15th.

data.title: Power Spark is about to launch an AI-powered trading big data model, aiming to build intelligent trading infrastructure.
data.description: Power Spark is about to launch an AI-powered trading model, aiming to build intelligent trading infrastructure. This model is not a traditional quantitative tool, but rather a foundational model system built for real-world assets and on-chain trading scenarios, focusing on key aspects such as market trend analysis, trading decision support, and risk control. This model will serve the long-term stable operation of the trading and liquidity system as an ecosystem-level capability. The Power Spark team stated that the upcoming AI is a crucial foundational capability for the next generation of trading systems.

According to on-chain analytics firm Token Terminal, this […]

data.title: BlackRock CEO Predicts U.S. Economic Growth Above Trend
data.description: According to Odaily, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink has projected that the U.S. economy will experience growth above the trend level in the coming years. Fink also noted that current investment conditions are safer compared to a year ago.

data.title: Silver's market capitalization surpasses $5 trillion, once again surpassing Nvidia to become the world's second-largest asset.
data.description: According to data from 8marketcap, silver's market capitalization has surpassed $5 trillion, currently standing at $5.037 trillion, once again exceeding Nvidia's market capitalization to become the world's second-largest asset by market value, second only to gold (currently valued at $32.071 trillion). Previously, on January 7th, silver surpassed Nvidia to become the world's second-largest asset with a market capitalization of $4.63 trillion.

data.title: U.S. Stock Markets Open with Gains on January 15
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the Dow Jones Industrial Average opened on January 15 with an increase of 161.57 points, or 0.33%, reaching 49,311.2 points. The Nasdaq Composite Index rose by 208.7 points, or 0.89%, to 23,680.45 points. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 Index saw an uptick of 44.82 points, or 0.65%, opening at 6,971.42 points.

data.title: A whale withdrew over 1.97 billion MEME tokens from a Memeland-tagged address and deposited them into Binance.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about half an hour ago, a whale withdrew approximately 1.972 billion MEME tokens from the Memeland-tagged address and then deposited all of these tokens into Binance, worth approximately $2.28 million.

data.title: Bitcoin's Institutional Maturity and Future Price Predictions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ark Invest analyst and portfolio manager David Puell has highlighted a shift in Bitcoin's next phase, emphasizing that investor participation will depend more on the proportion of exposure they are willing to allocate and the investment tools they choose to engage with, rather than merely believing in the asset. With the introduction of spot Bitcoin ETFs in 2024 and the rapid development of digital asset treasury strategies, Bitcoin has crossed a significant threshold into institutional maturity.

The combined holdings of ETFs and digital asset treasuries now account for approximately 12% of Bitcoin's total supply, exceeding expectations and becoming a major driver of price trends by 2025, a trend likely to continue into 2026. As the amount of Bitcoin absorbed by ETFs and corporate treasuries surpasses expectations, the market is entering a more institutionalized phase with reduced volatility.

Ark Invest remains confident in its long-term valuation framework for Bitcoin. Based on Ark's published valuation model, predictions for Bitcoin's price by 2030 include approximately $300,000 in a bear market scenario, around $710,000 in a baseline scenario, and up to $1.5 million in a bull market scenario. David Puell stated that driven by the 'digital gold' narrative and institutional adoption, the company still expects Bitcoin to reach between $300,000 and $1.5 million by 2030.

Puell also noted that as volatility decreases and the extent of pullbacks narrows, Bitcoin may become increasingly attractive to investors with lower risk appetites in the next cycle.
